# PowerShell command lines (recon / encoded payloads)

**Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Pull the full `actor_process_command_line` for `powershell.exe` to spot recon (`whoami`, `hostname`), download cradles, or base64/`-EncodedCommand` payloads. Keep `causality_actor_process_image_name` to see what launched it (a PowerShell child of Office or a web browser is high-signal). For broad matching across pwsh variants, filter `actor_process_image_name in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")`.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and actor_process_image_name = "powershell.exe"
| fields _time, causality_actor_process_image_name, actor_process_command_line
```

_Live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (narrow 30-min window); measured ~0.0005 CU. Narrow the time window + filter early to keep real-dataset hunts cheap._
